assertions.title: Repo surface has no unexplained junk or artifact ballast
assertions.reason: The current repo surface keeps only still-referenced non-product areas: `artifacts/` is an active CI/gate output surface, `docs/CODE-RED/confidence-suite/TEST_CATALOG.json` is still consumed by gate scripts, `asyncloop` is absent, no root/docs worklog or report markdown files were found, and retained legacy docs/report surfaces are explicitly marked historical or non-canonical rather than posing as current product docs.
evidence: /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/.github/workflows/ci.yml:713,758,824,869 — CI uploads `artifacts/gate-fast/**`, `artifacts/gate-core/**`, `artifacts/gate-release/**`, and `artifacts/gate-reconciliation/**`, so the repo-root `artifacts/` surface is still live.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/scripts/gate_fast.sh:5,297; scripts/gate_core.sh:5,120; scripts/gate_release.sh:6,227; scripts/gate_reconciliation.sh:5,114; scripts/validate_test_catalog.py:18 — active scripts still write/read under `artifacts/` and consume `docs/CODE-RED/confidence-suite/TEST_CATALOG.json`.

Glob over `/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/*report*.md`, `*worklog*.md`, `docs/*report*.md`, and `docs/*worklog*.md` returned no matches, so there are no stray repo-root/docs-root worklog or report markdown files in canonical-looking locations.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/code-review/README.md:3 — the retained review-artifact tree is explicitly marked `NON-CANONICAL` and redirects readers to `docs/modules/`, `docs/flows/`, and `docs/INDEX.md`.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/flows/FLOW-INVENTORY.md:38,46-53 — historical `docs/workflows/` guides are kept only as reference and are clearly separated from the canonical flow packets.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/INDEX.md:107,151,157; docs/developer-guide.md:1-3; docs/endpoint-inventory.md:1-4; docs/frontend-handoff-finance.md:1-9; docs/accounting-portal-frontend-engineer-handoff.md:1-9 — retained legacy docs are explicitly labeled reference-only / non-canonical rather than presented as current product truth.
LS/Glob under `/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control` found no `asyncloop` path, matching the milestone requirement to remove that ballast once unreferenced.

assertions.title: Docs-only governance is narrow and consistent
assertions.reason: The executable classifier and canonical governance docs agree on the same narrow docs-only lane and on running `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` as the only validator while skipping Codex/subagent/runtime checks, but `mission.md` still says docs-only changes should `skip scrutiny and validators`. That omits the required lint-only check, so the documented policy is not fully consistent across mission/root/script surfaces.
evidence: /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/7b442fc0-ec79-409b-ba2e-1ff329abb078/mission.md:17 and 97-99 — docs-only changes are described as `skip scrutiny and validators`, with no lint-only exception.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/AGENTS.md:29-30 — repo governance narrows docs-only to the canonical docs/governance lane and says to run `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` only while skipping Codex review/subagent/runtime validators.
/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/CONVENTIONS.md:163,167-169 — canonical docs conventions repeat the same lane and the same lint-only + skip review/runtime behavior.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/agents/PERMISSIONS.md:17 — docs-only governance packets may skip Codex review/subagent review only when the lane stays inside the explicit allowlist and `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` passes.
/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/agents/WORKFLOW.md:18-22 — workflow governance repeats the same narrow lane and `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh`-only behavior.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/docs/SECURITY.md:23-24 — docs-only review-policy packets validate with `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh`, while runtime/config/schema/test changes use strict review checks.

/home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/scripts/enforce_codex_review_policy.sh:39,78,83 — the executable policy uses the same allowlist and emits `run knowledgebase lint only and skip Codex review/subagent/runtime validators`; non-doc paths fall back to strict review checks.
issues: Update mission-level docs-only guidance so it matches the executable policy: docs-only packets should be limited to the canonical allowlist and still run `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` rather than skipping validators entirely.

frictions.description: Initial repo-wide `Grep` sweeps were truncated because the repository contains many historical validation artifacts.
frictions.resolution: Narrowed searches to the specific governance files, scripts, and retained repo surfaces required by VAL-DOCS-005 and VAL-DOCS-006.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 1 passed and 1 failed. VAL-DOCS-005 passes because the remaining repo-root/docs-root non-product surfaces are still governance- or script-linked and clearly labeled when non-canonical. VAL-DOCS-006 fails because `mission.md` still says docs-only changes skip validators, which conflicts with the executable/docs governance rule that requires `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` as the sole docs-only validator.
